{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_393f9a0fb4ea41d9914c6dd4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR\n2\t\n3\t**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n4\t\n5\tARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n6\t\n7\t- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n8\t- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n9\t- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n10\t- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n11\t- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n12\t\n13\t## Quick Start\n14\t\n15\t### Docker\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\tdocker run -d \\\n19\t  -p 9000:9000 \\\n20\t  -p 9001:9001 \\\n21\t  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n22\t  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n23\t  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n24\t  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n25\t  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n26\t  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n27\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n28\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n29\t  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\t> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n33\t\n34\t### Client Configuration\n35\t\n36\tPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n37\t\n38\t```bash\n39\t# AWS CLI\n40\taws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n41\t\n42\t# boto3 (Python)\n43\timport boto3\n44\ts3 = boto3.client('s3',\n45\t    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n46\t    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n47\t    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\n48\ts3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n49\t\n50\t# DuckDB\n51\tINSTALL httpfs;\n52\tLOAD httpfs;\n53\tSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\n54\tSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\n55\tSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\n56\tSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\t## Cost Model\n60\t\n61\t| Component | Cost |\n62\t|-----------|------|\n63\t| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n64\t| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n65\t| B2 API calls | $0 |\n66\t| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n67\t| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n68\t\n69\t## Architecture\n70\t\n71\t### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\t┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n75\t│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n76\t│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n77\t│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n78\t└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n82\t\n83\t```\n84\t┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n85\t│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n86\t│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n87\t│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n88\t└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n89\t```\n90\t\n91\t### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n92\t\n93\tDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n94\t\n95\t```\n96\tDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n97\t  │                                │                              │\n98\t  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n99\t  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n100\t  │                                │                              │\n101\t  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n102\t  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n103\t  │                                │                              │\n104\t  └─ result set                    └                              └\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t## Encryption Design\n108\t\n109\t```\n110\tMaster Key (MEK)\n111\t │  stored locally, never uploaded\n112\t │\n113\t └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n114\t                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n115\t                │\n116\t                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n117\t                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n118\t                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n119\t                                   seekable random access\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\tKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n123\t\n124\t## Security Model\n125\t\n126\t| Threat | Mitigation |\n127\t|--------|-----------|\n128\t| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n129\t| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n130\t| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n131\t| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n132\t| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n133\t| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\tARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n140\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n141\t| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n142\t| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n143\t| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n144\t| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n145\t| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n146\t| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n147\t| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n148\t| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n149\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n150\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n151\t| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n152\t| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n153\t| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n154\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n155\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n156\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n157\t\n158\t### Multi-Key Routing\n159\t\n160\tRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n161\t\n162\t```bash\n163\tARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\n164\tARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\n165\tARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\n166\tARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Multi-Credential Configuration\n170\t\n171\t```bash\n172\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\n173\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\n174\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n175\t\n176\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\n177\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\n178\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t## S3 API Coverage\n182\t\n183\t### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n184\t\n185\t| Operation | Support |\n186\t|-----------|---------|\n187\t| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n188\t| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n189\t| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n190\t| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n191\t| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n192\t| UploadPart | Full |\n193\t| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n194\t| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n195\t| ListParts | Full |\n196\t| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n197\t\n198\t### Passthrough Operations\n199\t\n200\t| Operation | Support |\n201\t|-----------|---------|\n202\t| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n203\t| DeleteObject | Full |\n204\t| DeleteObjects | Full |\n205\t| ListBuckets | Full |\n206\t| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n207\t| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n208\t| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n209\t\n210\t**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n211\t\n212\tThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n213\t\n214\t- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n215\t- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n216\t- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n217\t- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n218\t- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n219\t- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n220\t- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n221\t\n222\tInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n223\t\n224\t## Web Dashboard\n225\t\n226\tA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n227\t\n228\t```bash\n229\topen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n230\t```\n231\t\n232\tFeatures:\n233\t- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n234\t- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n235\t- Metadata cache hit rates\n236\t- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n237\t\n238\tSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n239\t\n240\t## Admin API\n241\t\n242\tKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n243\t\n244\t| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n245\t|----------|--------|-------------|\n246\t| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n247\t| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n248\t| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n249\t| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n250\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n251\t| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n252\t| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n253\t| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n254\t| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n255\t| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n256\t\n257\t## Repository Structure\n258\t\n259\t```\n260\tARMOR/\n261\t├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n262\t├── internal/\n263\t│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n264\t│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n265\t│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n266\t│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n267\t│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n268\t│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n269\t│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n270\t│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n271\t│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n272\t│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n273\t│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n274\t├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n275\t├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n276\t└── docs/\n277\t    ├── dashboard.md\n278\t    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n279\t    └── research/\n280\t```\n281\t\n282\t## Documentation\n283\t\n284\t- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n285\t- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n286\t- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n287\t- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n288\t\n289\t## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n290\t\n291\tARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n292\t\n293\t- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n294\t- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n295\t\n296\t### Building the Decrypt Tool\n297\t\n298\t```bash\n299\tgo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n300\t```\n301\t\n302\t### Usage\n303\t\n304\t#### Decrypt from B2\n305\t\n306\t```bash\n307\t# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\n308\tarmor-decrypt \\\n309\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n310\t  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n311\t  -output recovered-file.txt\n312\t\n313\t# Using MEK from environment\n314\texport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\n315\tarmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n316\t\n317\t# With verbose output\n318\tarmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n319\t```\n320\t\n321\t#### Decrypt from Local File\n322\t\n323\tFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n324\t\n325\t```bash\n326\tarmor-decrypt \\\n327\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n328\t  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n329\t  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n330\t  -output plaintext.bin\n331\t```\n332\t\n333\t### Key Requirements\n334\t\n335\t- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n336\t- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n337\t- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n338\t\n339\t### Multi-Key Support\n340\t\n341\tIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n342\t\n343\t```bash\n344\tarmor-decrypt \\\n345\t  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n346\t  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n347\t  -key-id sensitive \\\n348\t  -output recovered.txt\n349\t```\n350\t\n351\tThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n352\t\n353\t### Verification\n354\t\n355\tThe decrypt tool automatically:\n356\t\n357\t- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n358\t- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n359\t- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n360\t\n361\tExit codes:\n362\t- `0`: Success\n363\t- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n364\t\n365\t### Example Workflow\n366\t\n367\t```bash\n368\t# 1. List objects to find the target\n369\taws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n370\t\n371\t# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\n372\taws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n373\t  --bucket bucket --key file\n374\t\n375\t# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\n376\tarmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n377\t\n378\t# 4. Verify the recovered file\n379\tsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n380\t```\n381\t\n382\t## License\n383\t\n384\tMIT\n385\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56e12aa1-ee56-4423-a440-4361ebfa61ce","uuid":"984e77d6-23fc-4708-9726-1a522b243208","timestamp":"2026-07-15T16:22:00.149Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/README.md","content":"# ARMOR\n\n**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n\nARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n\n- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker run -d \\\n  -p 9000:9000 \\\n  -p 9001:9001 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n```\n\n> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n\n### Client Configuration\n\nPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n\n```bash\n# AWS CLI\naws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n\n# boto3 (Python)\nimport boto3\ns3 = boto3.client('s3',\n    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\ns3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n\n# DuckDB\nINSTALL httpfs;\nLOAD httpfs;\nSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\nSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\nSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\nSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n```\n\n## Cost Model\n\n| Component | Cost |\n|-----------|------|\n| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n| B2 API calls | $0 |\n| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n\nDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n\n```\nDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  └─ result set                    └                              └\n```\n\n## Encryption Design\n\n```\nMaster Key (MEK)\n │  stored locally, never uploaded\n │\n └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n                │\n                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n                                   seekable random access\n```\n\nKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n\n## Security Model\n\n| Threat | Mitigation |\n|--------|-----------|\n| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n\n## Configuration\n\nARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n\n### Multi-Key Routing\n\nRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n\n```bash\nARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\nARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\nARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\nARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n```\n\n### Multi-Credential Configuration\n\n```bash\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n```\n\n## S3 API Coverage\n\n### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n| UploadPart | Full |\n| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n| ListParts | Full |\n| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n\n### Passthrough Operations\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n| DeleteObject | Full |\n| DeleteObjects | Full |\n| ListBuckets | Full |\n| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n\n**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n\nThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n\n- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n\nInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n\n## Web Dashboard\n\nA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n```bash\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\nFeatures:\n- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n- Metadata cache hit rates\n- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n\nSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n\n## Admin API\n\nKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n\n## Repository Structure\n\n```\nARMOR/\n├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n├── internal/\n│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n└── docs/\n    ├── dashboard.md\n    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n    └── research/\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n\n## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n\nARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n\n- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n\n### Building the Decrypt Tool\n\n```bash\ngo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n```\n\n### Usage\n\n#### Decrypt from B2\n\n```bash\n# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n  -output recovered-file.txt\n\n# Using MEK from environment\nexport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\narmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n\n# With verbose output\narmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n```\n\n#### Decrypt from Local File\n\nFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n  -output plaintext.bin\n```\n\n### Key Requirements\n\n- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n\n### Multi-Key Support\n\nIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n  -key-id sensitive \\\n  -output recovered.txt\n```\n\nThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n\n### Verification\n\nThe decrypt tool automatically:\n\n- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n\nExit codes:\n- `0`: Success\n- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n\n### Example Workflow\n\n```bash\n# 1. List objects to find the target\naws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n\n# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\naws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n  --bucket bucket --key file\n\n# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\narmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n\n# 4. Verify the recovered file\nsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n```\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":385,"startLine":1,"totalLines":385}}}

credentials,\n44\t\t\tregion:      region,\n45\t\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n46\t\t}\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\n50\ttype AuthHeader struct {\n51\t\tAlgorithm     string\n52\t\tAccessKey     string\n53\t\tCredentialDate string\n54\t\tRegion        string\n55\t\tService       string\n56\t\tSignedHeaders []string\n57\t\tSignature     string\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n61\t// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\n62\tfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n63\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n64\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n68\t\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n69\t\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n70\t\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n71\t\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n72\t\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n73\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n74\t\t\t}\n75\t\t}\n76\t\n77\t\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n78\t\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n82\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n83\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n84\t\t\t\tcontinue\n85\t\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n88\t\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n89\t\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n90\t\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n91\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n92\t\t\t\t}\n93\t\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n94\t\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n95\t\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n96\t\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n97\t\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n98\t\n99\t\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n100\t\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n101\t\n102\t\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n103\t\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n104\t\t\t}\n105\t\t}\n106\t\n107\t\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n108\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n109\t\t}\n110\t\n111\t\treturn result, nil\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\t// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n115\t// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\n116\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n117\t\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n118\t\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n119\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n123\t\tif err != nil {\n124\t\t\treturn nil, err\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Look up credential by access key\n128\t\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n129\t\tif !exists {\n130\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n131\t\t}\n132\t\n133\t\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n134\t\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n135\t\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n136\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n137\t\t}\n138\t\n139\t\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n140\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n141\t\tif err != nil {\n142\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n143\t\t}\n144\t\n145\t\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n146\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Build canonical request\n150\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n151\t\n152\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n153\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n154\t\n155\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n156\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n157\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n158\t\n159\t\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n160\t\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n161\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n162\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\treturn cred, nil\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\n168\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n169\t\t// 1. HTTP method\n170\t\tmethod := r.Method\n171\t\n172\t\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n173\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n174\t\tif path == \"\" {\n175\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n176\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t// 3. Canonical query string\n179\t\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n180\t\n181\t\t// 4. Canonical headers\n182\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n183\t\n184\t\t// 5. Signed headers\n185\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n186\t\n187\t\t// 6. Hashed payload\n188\t\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n189\t\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n190\t\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n191\t\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n192\t\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n193\t\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n194\t\t}\n195\t\n196\t\t// Combine\n197\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n198\t\t\tmethod,\n199\t\t\tpath,\n200\t\t\tquery,\n201\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n202\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n203\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n204\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\n208\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n209\t\tif len(query) == 0 {\n210\t\t\treturn \"\"\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\t// Sort keys\n214\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n215\t\tfor k := range query {\n216\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n217\t\t}\n218\t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n219\t\n220\t\tvar parts []string\n221\t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n222\t\t\t// Sort values for each key\n223\t\t\tvalues := query[k]\n224\t\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n225\t\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n226\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\n234\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n235\t\tvar lines []string\n236\t\n237\t\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n238\t\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n239\t\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n240\t\t\t\thost := r.Host\n241\t\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n242\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n246\t\t\t\tcontinue\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\n249\t\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n250\t\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n251\t\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n252\t\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n253\t\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n254\t\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\tcontinue\n259\t\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n262\t\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n263\t\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n264\t\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n265\t\t\t}\n266\t\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\n273\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n274\t\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n275\t\n276\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n277\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n278\t\t\tamzDate,\n279\t\t\tcredentialScope,\n280\t\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n281\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\n285\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n286\t\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n287\t\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n288\t\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n289\t\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n290\t\treturn kSigning\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n294\t// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\n295\tfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n296\t\t// No ACLs means full access\n297\t\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n298\t\t\treturn nil\n299\t\t}\n300\t\n301\t\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n302\t\t\t// Check bucket match\n303\t\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n304\t\t\t\tcontinue\n305\t\t\t}\n306\t\n307\t\t\t// Check prefix match\n308\t\t\tif acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n309\t\t\t\t// Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n310\t\t\t\treturn nil\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n313\t\t\t\treturn nil\n314\t\t\t}\n315\t\t}\n316\t\n317\t\treturn ErrAccessDenied\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\t// hmacSHA256 computes HMAC-SHA256.\n321\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n322\t\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n323\t\th.Write([]byte(data))\n324\t\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\n328\tfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n329\t\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\n333\tfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n334\t\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n335\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// Authentication errors\n339\tvar (\n340\t\tErrMissingAuthHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"}\n341\t\tErrInvalidAlgorithm    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAlgorithm\", Message: \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\"}\n342\t\tErrInvalidCredential   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidCredential\", Message: \"Invalid credential format\"}\n343\t\tErrMissingFields       = &AuthError{Code: \"IncompleteSignature\", Message: \"Authorization header is missing required fields\"}\n344\t\tErrInvalidAccessKey    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", Message: \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\"}\n345\t\tErrMissingDateHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingDateHeader\", Message: \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\"}\n346\t\tErrInvalidDateFormat   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidDateFormat\", Message: \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\"}\n347\t\tErrRequestExpired      = &AuthError{Code: \"RequestExpired\", Message: \"Request has expired\"}\n348\t\tErrSignatureMismatch   = &AuthError{Code: \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", Message: \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\"}\n349\t\tErrAccessDenied        = &AuthError{Code: \"AccessDenied\", Message: \"Access Denied\"}\n350\t)\n351\t\n352\t// AuthError represents an authentication error.\n353\ttype AuthError struct {\n354\t\tCode    string\n355\t\tMessage string\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\tfunc (e *AuthError) Error() string {\n359\t\treturn e.Message\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t// VerifyQueryAuth verifies SigV4 authentication via query parameters (presigned URLs).\n363\t// Returns the credential if verification succeeds.\n364\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n365\t\t// Extract query parameters\n366\t\tquery := r.URL.Query()\n367\t\n368\t\taccessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n369\t\tif accessKeyCred == \"\" {\n370\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n371\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t// Parse credential\n374\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(accessKeyCred, \"/\")\n375\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n376\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\t// Look up credential by access key\n380\t\tcred, exists := a.credentials[credParts[0]]\n381\t\tif !exists {\n382\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n383\t\t}\n384\t\n385\t\t// Get signature from query\n386\t\tsignature := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Signature\")\n387\t\tif signature == \"\" {\n388\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n389\t\t}\n390\t\n391\t\t// Get date\n392\t\tamzDate := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n393\t\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n394\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n395\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\t// Get signed headers\n398\t\tsignedHeadersStr := query.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n399\t\tif signedHeadersStr == \"\" {\n400\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n401\t\t}\n402\t\tsignedHeaders := strings.Split(signedHeadersStr, \";\")\n403\t\n404\t\t// Get expires and verify\n405\t\texpires := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Expires\")\n406\t\tif expires != \"\" {\n407\t\t\texpiresSec, err := strconv.Atoi(expires)\n408\t\t\tif err != nil {\n409\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n410\t\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n413\t\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n415\t\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t\tif time.Since(requestTime) > time.Duration(expiresSec)*time.Second {\n418\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n419\t\t\t}\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// For query auth, the body is typically empty for GET requests\n423\t\tbody := []byte{}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Build canonical request (excluding signature from query)\n426\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r, signedHeaders, body, query)\n427\t\n428\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n429\t\tcredentialDate := credParts[1]\n430\t\tclientRegion := credParts[2]\n431\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, clientRegion, canonicalRequest)\n432\t\n433\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n434\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n435\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n436\t\n437\t\tif calculatedSig != signature {\n438\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn cred, nil\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// buildCanonicalQueryRequest builds canonical request for query-based auth.\n445\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte, query url.Values) string {\n446\t\t// Create a copy of query params without auth-related ones\n447\t\tcanonicalQuery := make(url.Values)\n448\t\tfor k, v := range query {\n449\t\t\t// Skip auth-related params\n450\t\t\tif k == \"X-Amz-Signature\" {\n451\t\t\t\tcontinue\n452\t\t\t}\n453\t\t\tcanonicalQuery[k] = v\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\t// Build canonical query string\n457\t\tqueryStr := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(canonicalQuery)\n458\t\n459\t\t// Build canonical request\n460\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n461\t\tif path == \"\" {\n462\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n466\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n467\t\tpayloadHash := sha256Sum(body)\n468\t\n469\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n470\t\t\tr.Method,\n471\t\t\tpath,\n472\t\t\tqueryStr,\n473\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n474\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n475\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n476\t\t}, Package server implements the ARMOR S3-compatible HTTP server.\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"crypto/hmac\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n)\n\n// SigV4Auth handles AWS Signature Version 4 authentication.\ntype SigV4Auth struct {\n\tcredentials map[string]*config.Credential // access key -> credential\n\tregion      string\n\tservice     string\n}\n\n// NewSigV4Auth creates a new SigV4 authenticator with a single credential.\nfunc NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\taccessKey: {\n\t\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n\t\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil, // nil means full access\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tregion:  region,\n\t\tservice: \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials creates a SigV4 authenticator with multiple credentials.\nfunc NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(credentials map[string]*config.Credential, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: credentials,\n\t\tregion:      region,\n\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\ntype AuthHeader struct {\n\tAlgorithm     string\n\tAccessKey     string\n\tCredentialDate string\n\tRegion        string\n\tService       string\n\tSignedHeaders []string\n\tSignature     string\n}\n\n// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\nfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n\t}\n\n\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n\n\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n\n\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up credential by access key\n\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n\t}\n\n\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n\t}\n\n\t// Build canonical request\n\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n\n\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n\n\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n\n\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n\t}\n\n\treturn cred, nil\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n\t// 1. HTTP method\n\tmethod := r.Method\n\n\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\tpath = \"/\"\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. Canonical query string\n\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n\n\t// 4. Canonical headers\n\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n\n\t// 5. Signed headers\n\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n\n\t// 6. Hashed payload\n\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\tmethod,\n\t\tpath,\n\t\tquery,\n\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n\t\tpayloadHash,\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n\tif len(query) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort keys\n\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n\tfor k := range query {\n\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n\t}\n\tsort.Strings(keys)\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, k := range keys {\n\t\t// Sort values for each key\n\t\tvalues := query[k]\n\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n\t\t\thost := r.Host\n\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n}\n\n// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\tamzDate,\n\t\tcredentialScope,\n\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n\treturn kSigning\n}\n\n// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\nfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n\t// No ACLs means full access\n\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n\t\t// Check bucket match\n\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check prefix match\n\t\tif acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ErrAccessDenied\n}\n\n// hmacSHA256 computes HMAC-SHA256.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n\th.Write([]byte(data))\n\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n}\n\n// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\nfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n}\n\n// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\nfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n}\n\n// Authentication errors\nvar (\n\tErrMissingAuthHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"}\n\tErrInvalidAlgorithm    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAlgorithm\", Message: \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\"}\n\tErrInvalidCredential   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidCredential\", Message: \"Invalid credential format\"}\n\tErrMissingFields       = &AuthError{Code: \"IncompleteSignature\", Message: \"Authorization header is missing required fields\"}\n\tErrInvalidAccessKey    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", Message: \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\"}\n\tErrMissingDateHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingDateHeader\", Message: \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\"}\n\tErrInvalidDateFormat   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidDateFormat\", Message: \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\"}\n\tErrRequestExpired      = &AuthError{Code: \"RequestExpired\", Message: \"Request has expired\"}\n\tErrSignatureMismatch   = &AuthError{Code: \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", Message: \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\"}\n\tErrAccessDenied        = &AuthError{Code: \"AccessDenied\", Message: \"Access Denied\"}\n)\n\n// AuthError represents an authentication error.\ntype AuthError struct {\n\tCode    string\n\tMessage string\n}\n\nfunc (e *AuthError) Error() string {\n\treturn e.Message\n}\n\n// VerifyQueryAuth verifies SigV4 authentication via query parameters (presigned URLs).\n// Returns the credential if verification succeeds.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\t// Extract query parameters\n\tquery := r.URL.Query()\n\n\taccessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n\tif accessKeyCred == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse credential\n\tcredParts := strings.Split(accessKeyCred, \"/\")\n\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up credential by access key\n\tcred, exists := a.credentials[credParts[0]]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n\t}\n\n\t// Get signature from query\n\tsignature := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Signature\")\n\tif signature == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, time.Duration(expiresSec)*time.Second {\n\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// For query auth, the body is typically empty for GET requests\n\tbody := []byte{}\n\n\t// Build canonical request (excluding signature from query)\n\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r, signedHeaders, body, query)\n\n\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n\tcredentialDate := credParts[1]\n\tclientRegion := credParts[2]\n\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, clientRegion, canonicalRequest)\n\n\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n\tcalculatedSig := one trailing slash\n301\t// - Empty string stays empty (no prefix)\n302\t// Examples:\n303\t//   \"\" → \"\"\n304\t//   \"kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n305\t//   \"kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n306\t//   \"/kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n307\t//   \"/kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n308\t//   \"kalshi-tape//\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n309\tfunc normalizePrefix(prefix trailing slashes first\n318\t\tprefix = strings.TrimRight(prefix, \"/\")\n319\t\n320\t\t// Add exactly one trailing slash if non-empty\n321\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n322\t\t\tprefix += \"/\"\n323\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\treturn prefix\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\tfunc generateRandomKey(length int) string {\n329\t\tb := make([]byte, length)\n330\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {\n331\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to generate random key: %v\", err))\n332\t\t}\n333\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(b)\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// parseKeyRoutes parses a key routes string.\n337\t// Format: \"prefix1=key1,prefix2=key2,*=default\"\n338\t// The * prefix is a catch-all that maps to the default key.\n339\tfunc parseKeyRoutes(routesStr string) ([]KeyRoute, error) {\n340\t\tif routesStr == \"\" {\n341\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n342\t\t}\n343\t\n344\t\tvar routes []KeyRoute\n345\t\tparts := strings.Split(routesStr, \",\")\n346\t\n347\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n348\t\t\tpart doesn't conflict with default credential names.\n382\tfunc loadNamedCredentials(cfg *Config) error {\n383\t\t// Collect all credential names\n384\t\tcredNames := make(map[string]bool)\n385\t\tfor _, {\n391\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t\t// Extract name: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY -> <NAME>\n394\t\t\t\tenvKey := parts[0]\n395\t\t\t\t// Skip the default credential env \"_SECRET_KEY\")\n412\t\t\taclStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_ACL\")\n413\t\n414\t\t\tif accessKey == \"\" || secretKey == \"\" {\n415\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_AUTH_%s_ACCESS_KEY cred\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\treturn nil\n441\t}\n442\t\n443\t// parseACL parses an ACL string into ACL entries.\n444\t// Format: \"bucket1:prefix1,bucket2:prefix2,bucket3:*\"\n445\t// A bucket of \"*\" means all buckets.\n446\t// A prefix of \"*\" or \"\" means any prefix within the bucket.\n447\tfunc parseACL(aclStr string) ([]ACLEntry, error) {\n448\t\tif aclStr == \"\" {\n449\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tvar entries []ACLEntry\n453\t\tparts := strings.Split(aclStr, \",\")\n454\t\n455\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n456\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n457\t\t\tif {\n476\t\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n477\t\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\t\tentries = append(entries, ACLEntry{\n480\t\t\t\tBucket: bucket,\n481\t\t\t\tPrefix: prefix,\n482\t\t\t})\n483\t\t}\n484\t\n485\t\tif (ADR-001)\n\t// Normalize to exactly one trailing slash, no leading slash\n\tcfg.Prefix = normalizePrefix(os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PREFIX\"))\n\n\t// Canary disabled flag\n\tcfg.CanaryDisabled = required\")\n\t}\n\tvar err error\n\tcfg.MEK, err = hex.DecodeString(mekHex)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK must be hex-encoded: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif len(cfg.MEK) != 32 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK must be 32 bytes (64 hex chars), got %d bytes\", len(cfg.MEK))\n\t}\n\n\t// Block size (default 64KB)\n\tcfg.BlockSize = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE\", 65536)\n\tif cfg.BlockSize < 4096 || (cfg.BlockSize&(cfg.BlockSize-1)) != 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE must be a power of 2 >= 4096\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Auth credentials (generate random if not provided)\n\tcfg.AuthAccessKey = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY\")\n\tif cfg.AuthAccessKey == \"\" {\n\t\tcfg.AuthAccessKey = generateRandomKey(16)\n\t}\n\tcfg.AuthSecretKey = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY\")\n\tif cfg.AuthSecretKey == \"\" {\n\t\tcfg.AuthSecretKey = generateRandomKey(32)\n\t}\n\n\t// Initialize credentials map with default credential\n\tcfg.Credentials = make(map[string]*Credential)\n\tcfg.Credentials[cfg.AuthAccessKey] = &Credential{\n\t\tAccessKey: cfg.AuthAccessKey,\n\t\tSecretKey: cfg.AuthSecretKey,\n\t\tACLs:      nil, // nil means full access to configured bucket\n\t}\n\n\t// Load additional named credentials (ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY, _SECRET_KEY, _ACL)\n\tif err := loadNamedCredentials(cfg); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Writer ID (default to hostname)\n\tcfg.WriterID = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_WRITER_ID\")\n\tif cfg.WriterID == \"\" {\n\t\tcfg.WriterID, _ = os.Hostname()\n\t\tif cfg.WriterID == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcfg.WriterID = \"armor-unknown\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Cache configuration\n\tcfg.CacheMaxEntries = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES\", 10000)\n\tcfg.CacheTTL = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_CACHE_TTL\", 300)\n\n\t// List cache configuration\n\tcfg.ListCacheMaxEntries = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES\", 1000)\n\tcfg.ListCacheTTL = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_TTL\", 60)\n\n\t// Readiness probe configuration\n\tcfg.ReadyzCacheTTL = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL\", 30)\n\n\t// Manifest index configuration\n\tmanifestEnabledStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_ENABLED\")\n\tcfg.ManifestEnabled = manifestEnabledStr != \"false\" && manifestEnabledStr != \"0\"\n\tcfg.ManifestPrefix = getEnv(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_PREFIX\", \".armor/manifest\")\n\tcfg.ManifestCompactionInterval = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_COMPACTION_INTERVAL\", 3600)\n\tcfg.ManifestCompactionThreshold = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_COMPACTION_THRESHOLD\", 1000)\n\n\t// Pre-signed URL configuration\n\tpresignSecretHex := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET\")\n\tif presignSecretHex != \"\" {\n\t\tcfg.PresignSecret, err = hex.DecodeString(presignSecretHex)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be hex-encoded: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(cfg.PresignSecret) < 32 {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be at least 32 bytes (64 hex chars)\")\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Use the auth secret key as the presign secret if not specified\n\t\tcfg.PresignSecret = []byte(cfg.AuthSecretKey)\n\t}\n\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_BASE_URL\")\n\tif cfg.PresignBaseURL == \"\" {\n\t\t// Default to /share path on the main listener\n\t\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = \"/share\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Load named keys (ARMOR_MEK_<NAME>)\n\tcfg.NamedKeys = make(map[string][]byte)\n\tfor _, env := range os.Environ() {\n\t\t// Look for ARMOR_MEK_<NAME> pattern\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, \"ARMOR_MEK_\") {\n\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(env, \"=\", 2)\n\t\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tname := strings.TrimPrefix(parts[0], \"ARMOR_MEK_\")\n\t\t\tname = strings.ToLower(name)\n\t\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Decode hex MEK\n\t\t\tmek, err := hex.DecodeString(parts[1])\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be hex-encoded: %w\", name, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif len(mek) != 32 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be 32 bytes (64 hex chars), got %d bytes\", name, len(mek))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcfg.NamedKeys[name] = mek\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Load key routes (ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES)\n\tif routesStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES\"); routesStr != \"\" {\n\t\troutes, err := parseKeyRoutes(routesStr)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcfg.KeyRoutes = routes\n\t}\n\n\t// Dashboard authentication configuration\n\tcfg.DashboardUser = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER\")\n\tcfg.DashboardPass = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS\")\n\tcfg.DashboardToken = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN\")\n\n\treturn cfg, nil\n}\n\nfunc getEnv(key, defaultVal string) string {\n\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n\t\treturn val\n\t}\n\treturn defaultVal\n}\n\nfunc getEnvInt(key string, defaultVal int) int {\n\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n\t\tif i, err := strconv.Atoi(val); err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn i\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn defaultVal\n}\n\n// normalizePrefix normalizes a prefix string according to ADR-001.\n// - Removes leading slashes\n// - Ensures exactly one trailing slash\n// - Empty string stays empty (no prefix)\n// Examples:\n//   \"\" → \"\"\n//   \"kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n//   \"kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n//   \"/kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n//   \"/kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n//   \"kalshi-tape//\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\nfunc normalizePrefix(prefix string) string {\n\tif prefix == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove leading slashes\n\tprefix = strings.TrimLeft(prefix, \"/\")\n\n\t// Remove all trailing slashes first\n\tprefix = strings.TrimRight(prefix, \"/\")\n\n\t// Add exactly one trailing slash if non-empty\n\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\tprefix += \"/\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn prefix\n}\n\nfunc generateRandomKey(length int) string {\n\tb := make([]byte, length)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to generate random key: %v\", err))\n\t}\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n\n// parseKeyRoutes parses a key routes string.\n// Format: \"prefix1=key1,prefix2=key2,*=default\"\n// The * prefix is a catch-all that maps to the default key.\nfunc parseKeyRoutes(routesStr string) ([]KeyRoute, error) {\n\tif routesStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar routes []KeyRoute\n\tparts := strings.Split(routesStr, \",\")\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route format %q (expected prefix=keyname)\", part)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tprefix := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])\n\t\tkeyName := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])\n\n\t\tif prefix == \"\" || keyName == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route %q (empty prefix or key name)\", part)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Handle wildcard - it maps to default key\n\t\tif prefix == \"*\" {\n\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\troutes = append(routes, KeyRoute{\n\t\t\tPrefix:  prefix,\n\t\t\tKeyName: keyName,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn routes, nil\n}\n\n// loadNamedCredentials loads additional named credentials from environment variables.\n// Format: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY, ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_SECRET_KEY, ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACL\n// Named credentials must have a non-empty NAME that doesn't conflict with default credential names.\nfunc loadNamedCredentials(cfg *Config) error {\n\t// Collect all credential names\n\tcredNames := make(map[string]bool)\n\tfor _, env := range os.Environ() {\n\t\t// Look for ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY pattern where NAME is not empty\n\t\t// and not one of the default credential env vars\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, \"ARMOR_AUTH_\") && strings.Contains(env, \"_ACCESS_KEY=\") {\n\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(env, \"=\", 2)\n\t\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Extract name: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY -> <NAME>\n\t\t\tenvKey := parts[0]\n\t\t\t// Skip the default credential env var\n\t\t\tif envKey == \"ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tnamePart := strings.TrimPrefix(envKey, \"ARMOR_AUTH_\")\n\t\t\tnamePart = strings.TrimSuffix(namePart, \"_ACCESS_KEY\")\n\t\t\tif namePart == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcredNames[namePart] = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Load each credential\n\tfor name := range credNames {\n\t\taccessKey := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_ACCESS_KEY\")\n\t\tsecretKey := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_SECRET_KEY\")\n\t\taclStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_ACL\")\n\n\t\tif accessKey == \"\" || secretKey == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_AUTH_%s_ACCESS_KEY and ARMOR_AUTH_%s_SECRET_KEY are both required\", name, name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tcred := &Credential{\n\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Parse ACL if provided\n\t\tif aclStr != \"\" {\n\t\t\tacls, err := parseACL(aclStr)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_AUTH_%s_ACL: %w\", name, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcred.ACLs = acls\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for duplicate access key\n\t\tif _, exists := cfg.Credentials[accessKey]; exists {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate access key in ARMOR_AUTH_%s\", name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tcfg.Credentials[accessKey] = cred\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// parseACL parses an ACL string into ACL entries.\n// Format: \"bucket1:prefix1,bucket2:prefix2,bucket3:*\"\n// A bucket of \"*\" means all buckets.\n// A prefix of \"*\" or \"\" means any prefix within the bucket.\nfunc parseACL(aclStr string) ([]ACLEntry, error) {\n\tif aclStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar entries []ACLEntry\n\tparts := strings.Split(aclStr, \",\")\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Split bucket:prefix\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \":\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid ACL entry %q (expected bucket:prefix)\", part)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbucket := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])\n\t\tprefix := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])\n\n\t\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid ACL entry %q (empty bucket)\", part)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Normalize wildcard prefix\n\t\tif prefix == \"*\" {\n\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tentries = append(entries, ACLEntry{\n\t\t\tBucket: bucket,\n\t\t\tPrefix: prefix,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tif len(entries) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ACL string contains no valid entries\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn entries, for creating signed requests\n304\t\n305\tfunc createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t *testing.T, method, path, body, accessKey, secretKey string, timestamp *time.Time) *http.Request {\n306\t\tt.Helper()\n307\t\n308\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n309\t\tif timestamp != nil {\n310\t\t\tnow = *timestamp\n311\t\t}\n312\t\n313\t\tamzDate := formatAmzDate(now)\n314\t\tcredentialScope := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request\", amzDate[:8])\n315\t\n316\t\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n317\t\tif body != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tbodyBytes = []byte(body)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\n321\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bytes.NewReader(bodyBytes))\n322\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Host\", \"test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\")\n323\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", amzDate)\n324\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\", sha256Sum(bodyBytes))\n325\t\n326\t\tsignedHeaders := []string{\"host\", \"x-amz-content-sha256\", \"x-amz-date\"}\n327\t\tsort.Strings(signedHeaders)\n328\t\n329\t\tcanonicalRequest := buildCanonicalRequestForTest(req, signedHeaders, bodyBytes)\n330\t\tstringToSign := buildStringToSignForTest(amzDate, credentialScope, \"us-east-005\", canonicalRequest)\n331\t\tsigningKey := deriveSigningKey(secretKey, amzDate[:8], \"us-east-005\")\n332\t\tsignature := hex.EncodeToString(hmacSHA256ForTest(signingKey, stringToSign))\n333\t\n334\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s/%s, SignedHeaders=%s, Signature=%s\",\n335\t\t\taccessKey, credentialScope, strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\"), signature)\n336\t\n337\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n338\t\n339\t\treturn req\n340\t}\n341\t\n342\tfunc formatAmzDate(t time.Time) string {\n343\t\treturn t.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\tfunc buildCanonicalRequestForTest(req *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n347\t\tmethod := req.Method\n348\t\tpath := req.URL.EscapedPath()\n349\t\tif path == \"\" {\n350\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n351\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\tquery := req.URL.Query()\n354\t\tvar queryParts []string\n355\t\tif len(query) > 0 {\n356\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n357\t\t\tfor k := range query {\n358\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n359\t\t\t}\n360\t\t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n361\t\t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n362\t\t\t\tvalues := query[k]\n363\t\t\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n364\t\t\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n365\t\t\t\t\tqueryParts = append(queryParts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n366\t\t\t\t}\n367\t\t\t}\n368\t\t}\n369\t\n370\t\tvar headerLines []string\n371\t\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n372\t\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n373\t\t\t\thost := req.Host\n374\t\t\t\theaderLines = append(headerLines, \"host:\"+host)\n375\t\t\t\tcontinue\n376\t\t\t}\n377\t\t\tvalues := req.Header.Values(h)\n378\t\t\tif len(values) > 0 {\n379\t\t\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n380\t\t\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n381\t\t\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n382\t\t\t\t}\n383\t\t\t\theaderLines = append(headerLines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n384\t\t\t}\n385\t\t}\n386\t\tcanonicalHeaders := strings.Join(headerLines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n387\t\n388\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n389\t\tpayloadHash := req.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n390\t\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n391\t\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Hex(body)\n392\t\t}\n393\t\n394\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n395\t\t\tmethod,\n396\t\t\tpath,\n397\t\t\tstrings.Join(queryParts, \"&\"),\n398\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n399\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n400\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n401\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\tfunc buildStringToSignForTest(amzDate, credentialScope, region, h.Sum(nil)\n}\n\nfunc calculateQuerySignature(t *testing.T, auth *SigV4Auth, secretKey, amzDate, region string, req *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tcanonicalRequest
